title: Gravimetric Determination of Copper Using Thiosalicylic Acid
author: B. V. NARAYANA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, S. V. University, Tirupati-517502 

  Manuscript received 27 May 1974; accepted 7 August 1974  

 A gravimetric procedure for the determination of copper using thiosalicylic acid is reported. The method is found to be suitable for the determination of this metal in presence of zinc, iron, aluminium, nickel and cobalt. Copper in the range 10-80 mg. can be accurately determined by this procedure in the absence of these metals; whereas in their presence, copper upto 42 mg. can be determined accurately. This method can be conveniently applied in the analysis of brass.
